title: centaurea citricolor
intro: Centaurea citricolor, also known as the bitter thistle or sour knapweed, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its bitter compounds like sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea citricolor include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
